summary:
• Middle East geopolitical tensions—including ongoing Iran conflict and Chinese diplomatic silence on the Hormuz crisis—are creating immediate supply shortages with Brent crude breaking $111 and the EIA projecting a widening oil deficit in 2026.

• Unresolved conflicts and supply route disruptions in the Middle East have already cost global businesses $25 billion, raising risks that Western Hemisphere producers like Santos (Alaska Pikka) and shale developers in Argentina's Vaca Muerta will face sustained price volatility and demand uncertainties.

• Chinese refinery runs have fallen to 2022 lows despite Beijing's clean energy export ambitions, signaling weakening crude demand that could pressure Western Hemisphere export volumes even as U.S. upstream M&A activity ($38 billion) suggests domestic capital remains committed to production growth.
